UG-100  PNEUMATIC TEST. (a) When a hydrostatic test is not practicable, a pneumatic test may be applied, provided that the requirements of this paragraph are met. (b) The pneumatic test pressure shall be not less than 1.1 times the maximum allowable working pressure. (c) A preliminary test at a pressure not exceeding one-half the pneumatic test pressure shall be made first. Vessels shall meet the minimum wall thickness of UG-27. Because of the hazard of stored energy, adequate safety precautions shall be taken as required by UG-125. The test shall be witnessed by the Inspector per UG-93. For hydrostatic testing, see UG-99. See also UG-101 for proof tests as an alternative.
